title: Part 3
text: going missing under strange circumstances. It’s down to you to figure out whatever is causing the disturbance and put an end to it before more people disappear. Attacks The amount of harm a wound can cause is based on the creature's damage die: d4 = 0-1 harm, d6 = 2-3 harm, d8 = 4-5 harm, d10 = 6-7 harm, and d12 = 8+ harm. Harm Capacity To determine how much harm will kill a creature, double its Hit Protection. Armor remains unchanged. Weaknesses Most of the monsters and minions in the module are fungal based, sharing common weaknesses: fire, fungicide, and destruction of brain/spinal tissue. Treat the Children of Ammon (p. 9) as the primary monster for this adventure, with one main weakness: destroying thePortal (p. 27). Countdown For this module, the steps of the Doom Clock (p. 8) can be directly ported to the Countdown. CTHULHU DARK Framing The basis for the adventure, everyday folks getting caught up in a horror- filled mystery, can remain largely unchanged. However, The Mall leans much more heavily into natural/body horror than traditional cosmic horror, so some thematic changes to the system may be warranted. Investigators Use the Character Creation (p. 32) to determine your occupation. Insight Instead of Insight and an Insight Die, you have Infection and an Infection Die. Roll your Infection Die when you risk infection. Use Fallout (p. 40) as inspiration for your transformation. When Infection reaches 6, your character is Replaced (p. 8). When Infection reaches 5, you may attempt to reduce it by poisoning yourself with harsh medicines or fungicides, or amputating infected body parts. Fighting When you fight a Host, you are always risking infection. But if you try to defeat the Children of Ammon (p. 9) directly, you will die. Investigating When you investigate something, roll as normal. Use the demarcated Clues to aid. On a 6, instead of glimpsing beyond human knowledge, you learn hints that the Children of Ammon are much deeper rooted than it seems. Whisper Cards // The QR code links to different resources for bringing the cards into play. [37] WHISPER CARDS The Whisper Card mechanic is designed to push characters to action and allow PCs to be REPLACED. The contents of the cards are kept secret from the Facilitator and other players, leaving the rest of the table to question and wonder. At the end of the scene when the Child of Ammon first appears and kills an NPC erupts from (DOOM 2), give each player a Whisper Card and brief them on the mechanic. ▶These are Whisper Cards. ▶Individual players will get them from different actions and situations. ▶Whisper Cards can replace Stress for the acting character (and others in the party just receive Stress). ▶Each Whisper Card has two prompts. Choose one and act upon it in the subsequent scene(s). ▶Cards should be kept secret from other players. ▶Some Whisper Cards will indicate that your character has been REPLACED. While they will outwardly look the same (for now), their drives have changed. EXPLAINING THE MECHANIC EXAMPLE TRIGGERS ▶Taking Stress (only the most proactive player gets a card instead of Stress). ▶Triggers noted in the text. ▶Receiving Fallout. ▶Direct contact with spores. ▶Physical contact with fluids from a Child of Ammon. ▶Killing another person. ▶Touching the barrier. ▶The DOOM Clock progressing. ▶Complication for failing some saves. ▶Approaching the Portal (p. 25) reveals a special card addressing both the human and REPLACED characters. FORMATS There are multiple ways to implement Whisper cards at your table. You can print at home, have people roll on the tables or you can use the Digital Whisper Cards using PlayingCards.io Virtual Tabletop. All resources are linked in the QR code below and provided at purchase. In Person Cards ▶Print the cards and cutthem out. ▶At the beginning of the game, create your three Whisper decks, one for each tier of the story. ▶Shuffle one “REPLACED” card each into decks 2 & 3. These indicate changes have taken root in the PC which will influence their behavior. ▶Once you give out all the cards in a deck, move on to the next. ▶If you give out all cards, either create a new deck or roll on the below table to give out results. Using The Tables (in person or online) ▶When a player would get a card, have them roll a d12 and privately relay the information to the player. ▶This option does not keep the results a mystery from the Facilitator, but does not require any assembly or digital resources. ▶Each result can only be rolled once. ▶When all results have been rolled on a table, move to the next table. Virtual Tabletop ▶Download instructions and file for online play from the Liminal Horror Website or the QR code: [38] Whisper Cards // This inherently builds tension as the table is unsure who has been REPLACED. d12 Deck One (Whisper Cards) 01 Search for contaminants or Repeatedly check behind you 02 Inspect or Make erratic movements 03 Mourn or Hunker down 04 Show who is in charge or Break something 05 Search out Others or Reject 06 Take a risk or Be cautious 07 Arm yourself or Escalate 08 Reveal something or Lament 09 “We are on our own.” or Accuse someone 10 Gather resources or Focus on a detail 11 Demand or Reminisce 12Reroll d12 Deck Two (Whisper Cards) 01 Accuse someone or Take a risk 02 Forge an alliance or Remove an article of clothing 03 Oppose or Fortify 04 Give a command or “No one is coming for us.” 05 Withhold something or Abandon 06 Scheme or Fixate on a detail 07 Your body has been fully assimilated. You've been REPLACED. Lead the party deeper underground or put people in danger. 08 Search for contaminants or Show who is in charge 09 Get in someone's face or Search for evidence in the blood 10 Give a command or Protect someone 11 Abandon or Coordinate 12 Deflect or Search out others d12 Deck Two (Whisper Cards) 01 Scheme or Experience extreme déjà vu 02 Check for danger or Seek out normality 03 Arm yourself or Narrate your thoughts 04 Hide something or Make an accusation 05 Seek out food or Get in someone’s face 06 Oppose or Make an accusation 07 Your body has been fully assimilated. You've been REPLACED. Lead the party deeper underground or put people in danger. 08 Be cautious or Show who is in charge 09 Seek shelter or Coordinate 10 Reject or Forge an alliance 11 Demand proof or Seek out normality 12 Break something or Seek shelter Approaching The Portal (p. 27) card: As the party approaches the Portal, place this card in the center of the table. ▶ If you have not been REPLACED: You feel an immense sense of dread and wrongness when looking upon this portal. ▶ If you have been REPLACED: Transform and protect the portal. Quick Reference // A one-stop shop. [39] QUICK REFERENCE HAVE THEY BEEN REPLACED? To determine if an NPC has been REPLACED: ▶Roll a d10 against the DOOM level and make a CTRL save. � If the result is equal or under AND they fail a CTRL save, they have been REPLACED. ▶As the adventure progresses, NPCs’ CTRL may decrease. NPC REACTIONS When the Investigators encounter an NPC whose demeanor would not be obvious, roll 2d6 and consult the tables below. ▶2-4: Hostile ▶5-8: Wary/Paranoid ▶9-10: Curious ▶11: Kind ▶12: Helpful GENERIC NPC STATS STR: 10 DEX: 10 CTRL: 10 HP: 3 Fist (d4) or Improvised Weapon (d6) CHILD OF AMMON (p. 9) STR: 14 DEX: 13 CTRL 10 HP: 8 Armor: 1 ▶Damage: d6 - d12 (number of appendages, size, bones, fungus). ▶ Metamorphosis: Can change forms in moments, sometimes even breaking off into multiple unique instances. ▶ Anointed Blood: Spray a mix of blood, and spores. Witnesses take d4 Stress. ▶ Critical Damage: Fleshy tendrils pierce the victim’s flesh, worming their way between muscle and bone (D4 damage to DEX). DOOM CLOCK (p. 2) D1: Save the mall raffle. D2: Mall phases out. Monster attack. D3: Barrier has everyone trapped. D4: Factions emerge. D5: Child of Ammon caught eating. D6: Power goes out. D7: Supplies sabotaged. Fleshy mass- of-pets escapes pet shop. D8: Water scarsity. D9: Fungal Fields bloom throughout. D10: Mall phases back. PERSONNEL 1. Mr. Neil - Mall Manager (p. 6) 2. Billy - direct report to Mr. Neil (p. 6) 3. Kurt - Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 8) 4. Maggie - Head of the CBB (p. 6) 5. Griffith - Your Body is a Temple (p. 7) 6. Samuel - Griffith’s right hand (p. 7) 7. William- Head mall walker (p. 6) 8. Bruce - Maintenance (p. 8) 9. Terry - Night Security (p. 8) 10. Hudson - Head of Security (p. 7) 11. Beth - Head of the Mall Rats (p. 7) 12. Ruth - Patron (p. 7) 13. Emmy - Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 8) 14. Logan - DMV Express (p. 8) Whispers Cards (p. 37) Secret prompts triggered by character action (including every Fallout and often replacing Stress). A few cards cause a PC to become Replaced. Nightmare & Prophecies (p. 31) To sleep is to dream. All PCs make CTRL saves when sleeping. Failing means rolling on the Nightmare Table. APPENDIX N Carpenter, John. - The Thing Astron-6 - The Void Siebert, Maggie - Bonding Umezz, Kazuo - The Drifting Classroom THE MALL The Mall is a wet, gooey, practical effects filled TTRPG adventure set in a 1990s mall pulled out of time and space. The mall’s denizens are trapped inside with a creature not bound by any one form. It slithers among them now, preparing to assimilate and imitate its victims until no one is left and it can open a rift between its home dimension...AND EARTH! Content Warning: This module carries content warnings for death, human sacrifice, hallucinations, drugs, people trapped/imprisoned, instances of self-inflicted harm, temporal disorientation and body horror (bones and foreign objects breaking through skin, unwilling bodily transformation, bodily disfigurement). GOBLIN ARCHIVES’ PLACEHOLDER GOBLIN ARCHIVES
